question.text: Corporate bond trades executed in the Over-the-Counter (OTC) market must be reported on exchange reporting platforms within __________ of the trade execution.

answer.text: by end of the same trading day

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** SEBI mandates that all OTC trades in corporate bonds must be reported on exchange reporting platforms within 15 minutes of trade execution. This requirement enhances transparency in the corporate bond market, which has traditionally been opaque due to its OTC nature. The reporting platforms are operated by stock exchanges (NSE and BSE).
**The Trap:** 30 minutes was an older reporting timeline that has been tightened. 1 hour and end-of-day reporting would defeat the purpose of near-real-time transparency. Students may confuse corporate bond OTC reporting timelines with other reporting requirements.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Corporate bond market transparency measures are tested in NISM Series XII and are relevant for NISM V-A (Mutual Fund) since debt funds are major participants in the corporate bond market.
**Key Takeaway:** Corporate bond OTC trade reporting = within 15 minutes on exchange platforms. This is a transparency measure to make the OTC corporate bond market more visible.
